analysis:
# Coaching Report - 40 Second Match Clip

## RELIABLE INSIGHTS (team-level, high confidence)

The match had a stop-start rhythm with frequent interruptions. Play was broken up by dead balls at 9 seconds, 15-16 seconds, 17-18 seconds, and 24-31 seconds, creating choppy phases of open play.

Team shape shows a clear contrast: Team A maintained a compact 27.7m width with 10.5m depth, while Team B spread much wider at 44.4m width with similar 11.9m depth. This suggests different tactical approaches - one team playing narrow, the other stretched wide.

Only 4 players made sprints during this 40-second period: Player #15 (1 sprint), Player #7 (2 sprints), and Player #18 (1 sprint) from the reliable data.

Comparing to previous matches, Team A's current width of 27.7m is notably narrower than the 23.7m-36.0m range seen across previous matches, suggesting a more compact setup than usual.

## APPROXIMATE ESTIMATES (player-level, medium confidence)

Player #1236 made approximately 0-2 sprints with top speed around 32.3 km/h, covering roughly 30-50m.

Player #1604 was relatively inactive, making approximately 0-2 sprints at around 20.4 km/h, covering only 7-11m.

Player #608 showed moderate activity with approximately 0-3 sprints, reaching around 26.5 km/h over 3-5m of movement.

## DATA GAPS

Five players (#1052, #1055, #3352, #2093, #2603) had insufficient tracking data for reliable conclusions about their performance.

The 40-second clip provides limited context for full match analysis. Key tactical elements like ball possession, pass accuracy, and specific dead ball situations could not be measured from this data.

Several players (#13, #589, #2084) showed minimal or zero movement, which may indicate they were substitutes, injured players, or the tracking system lost them during this period.

## THE ONE THING TO ADDRESS BEFORE SATURDAY

Player #7 made 2 sprints and hit 31.3 km/h - the highest work rate among reliably tracked players. However, most of the team showed very low sprint counts (0-1) during open play phases. The lack of high-intensity running from the majority of players needs immediate attention for Saturday's match fitness.
